rationale.R1.5: The artifact has visible structural redundancy—the ablation study and the no-hyperparameter-tuning claim both appear in what reads as an introductory/abstract section, creating a fragmented narrative arc. 'Clean this up' plausibly extends to resolving this at the paragraph-ordering level, making R1.5 centrally relevant, though not yet confirmed as load-bearing.
rationale.R1.6: 'Clean this up' most directly targets sentence- and paragraph-level prose quality: tightening repetition, improving flow, and removing hedged or circular phrasing. This is load-bearing; failure to improve the prose invalidates the delegation.

parsed.clarification_question: Should 'clean this up' include structural reorganization—reordering or merging paragraphs to fix the narrative arc—or is it limited to sentence-level prose polish only?
